A NEW LOOK AT PERSUASION
‣ Persuasion beings with the words, 	“I understand.”
‣ 	Persuasion is a form of 	connection.
‣ Persuasion is an expression of 	empathy.
‣ Persuasion is the extension of two of the most basic human
needs: 	the need for hope and the need for connection.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 33 --

A NEW LOOK AT PERSUASION
‣ The rest is show and dazzle.
‣ 	Good copywriters all have higher than average EQ, or emotional
quotient. Great copywriters often score off the charts.
‣ Along with all kinds of 	linguistic goodies 	along the way…
‣ Master the art of that first sentence, “Persuasion begins with the
words, “I understand”, and you’ll 	change your life.

-- 17 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ What is empathy, and why is it 	crucial 	for copywriters to master?
‣ 	Empathy is best defined as 	the act of seeing, hearing, and
validating another person’s experience.
‣ Most copywriters write to sell, which is fine. However, they rarely
write with the solid and clear intention to 	see and hear 	their
prospect. Why?
‣ Seeing and hearing are 3-dimensional experiences.
‣ 	However, 	using words, you can convey the 	same 	emotional
and empathetic impact.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ It’s more important to know the 	customer than the product.
‣ 	Copywriters get their research backwards.
‣ While it’s important to know the product, the stats, the facts, it’s far
more important to know the mental framework 	of the buyer.
‣ That’s what The Empathy Exercise can give you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ What is “being seen”?
‣ 	Seen is accomplished when you 	understand and relate to both
their pain and potential.
‣ Pain 	in this context is life pain—the pain of loss, physical pain, and
the pain of disappointing oneself and one’s family and friends.
‣ Pain is 75% of persuasion. The other 25% is 	glorious hope.
‣ 	Seeing one’s pain involves specific words and phrases (covered
next), as well as one’s potential.
‣ Interview avatars and use 	their words!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ Step 1: Seeing, Part 1
‣ 	Write a list of your prospect’s greatest pains in life as they pertain to
the offer. Remember, you can always go 	one level deeper.
‣ For example, let’s take something ‘benign’ like internet marketing.
‣ Pains might be: lack of income, fear over going back to the real
world, and low open rates (blah, but important later!)
‣ 	Now let’s go 	deeper:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 33 --

White Belt:
“lack of income”
Samurai:
“creating fear in the mind of your children or family over barely paying the bills”
White Belt:
“fear over going back to the real world”
Samurai:
“admitting failure to everyone who said you’d never make it”
White Belt:
“low open rates”
Samurai:
“losing a potential buyer one lost click at a time…time and time again”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 33 --

NOTICE HOW OFTEN I START
WITH VERBS (IMPORTANT FOR LATER)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ These are the 	dark thoughts 	of your potential customer, and if you
spot them 	with acceptance, 	they feel totally seen…
‣ 	As long as you also see 	their potential. 	The dreams they still
harbor for their lives, which we’ll do in a moment.
‣ Step 2: Seeing, Part 2
‣ Now express that pain using 	visual words.
‣ 	This is an NLP trick to tap into the “vision center” of the brain.
‣ Example:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 33 --

“CAN YOU 	SEE 	THE FEAR IN YOUR
CHILDREN’S 	EYES 	AT NIGHT AS THEY
WORRY ABOUT YOU…AND ABOUT
THEIR FUTURE?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ What is “being heard”?
‣ 	Heard is defined as 	acknowledgement of frustration.
‣ In copy, hearing is a one-way street that is limited to the complaints
and frustrations of the reader.
‣ Hearing one’s frustration also involves specific words and phrases.
‣ 	Step 3: Hearing
‣ Write down the frustrations your prospect has.
‣ Then use 	hearing words 	to express them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ Examples: 	frustrated over lack of results, poor quality products or
service in the past, time constraints, feeling confused.
‣ 	Put into hearing words:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 33 --

“LISTEN, I GET THAT RESULTS IN THE PAST HAVE COME
FEW AND FAR BETWEEN. YOU 	HEAR 	ABOUT ONE
PRODUCT AFTER ANOTHER AND THE SUCCESS
OTHERS ARE HAVING. THAT WOULD MAKE
ANY SANE PERSON FEEL FRUSTRATED.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ What is “being validated”?
‣ 	Validation is defined as 	permission to feel anything.
‣ Most people feel bad about feeling bad, or feel angry about things
they think they should feel angry about.
‣ Validation assures them they’re not crazy. They’re not “weird”.
They’re not in danger.
‣ 	Validation creates a feeling of 	safety 	(vital for female readers.)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 33 --

THE EMPATHY EXERCISE
‣ Step 4: Validating
‣ 	Fill in every word and phrase you can think of to complete these
sentences. Write as many variations as possible. Think in terms of
feelings, hopes, desires, and disappointments…
‣ “If you _______ it makes perfect sense.”
‣ “Anyone would feel _____ about (problem) too.”
‣ 	“Wanting ________ is not just normal…it’s expected.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 33 --

LOVE LETTER MARKETING
‣ Love Letter Marketing involves three steps:
‣ 	1. 	Write to one person. 	Any phrases that sound written to groups
of people (with few exceptions) isn’t going to cut it. You should be
able to read this to 	one person you love 	and have them resonate.
‣ 2. 	Write to serve rather than sell. 	What do they need? What can
you give them right now? The words, 	“What do you need?” 	are
four of the most powerful in life, love, and marketing. Ask it point-
blank, followed by, 	“Will you let me help?”
‣ 3. 	Write with hope, 	but only after you empathize. This involves
future pacing (write as if the future is already present.)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 33 --

LOVE LETTER MARKETING
‣ Read my Love Letter essay and my introduction to Paula Abdul’s
autobiography.
‣ 	Spot and notate every instance where my words 	evoke emotion
rather than mere thinking.
‣ Write down what those emotions were.
‣ Notice the strong use of “you” in each. 	Aim for 10% of your
words to be “you” 	on long sales letters, and 5% within shorter
copy. This is my “10% You” Rule.
‣ 	Finally, write 	one sentence 	that inspires your reader to create 	a
legacy rather than a task.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
